---
title: "Moments loss function"
author: "Konrad Krämer"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
  pdf: default
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

### Moments

#### First Moment: the mean

\begin{equation}
E[X] = \bar{x} = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{i=1}^{N}x_i
\end{equation}

####  Raw second Moment

\begin{equation}
E[X^2] = \frac{1}{N}\sum_{j=1}^{N}x_{i}^{2}
\end{equation}

#### Centered second Moment

\begin{equation}
E[(X - \mu)^2] = Var(X) = E[X^2] - (E[X])^2
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
L \;=\;
\sum_g
\left[
\text{mean imbalance}
\;+\;
\lambda_{m2}\,\text{second moment imbalance}
\;+\;
\lambda_{\mathrm{cov}}\,\text{cross-moment imbalance}
\right]
\end{equation}

\begin{equation}
L \;=\;
\sum_{\substack{g=1 \\ g \neq \mathrm{UNUSED}}}^{G}

\left[

\sum_{j=1}^{C}
\left(\bar{x}_{g j}-\bar{x}_{\cdot j}\right)^2

\;+\;
\lambda_{m2}\sum_{j=1}^{C}
\left(\overline{x^{2}}_{g j}-\overline{x^{2}}_{\cdot j}\right)^2

\;+\;
\lambda_{\mathrm{cov}}\sum_{(a,b)\in{P}}
\left(\overline{x_a x_b}_{\,g}-\overline{x_a x_b}_{\,\cdot}\right)^2

\right].

\end{equation}

where:

* g = groups (Treatment, Control, etc.)
* G = total number of groups
* C = number of covariates (For instance: weights)
* P = number of pairs

\begin{equation}
P = \{(a,b) \mid 1 \le a < b \le p\},
\qquad
|P| = \binom{p}{2} = \frac{p(p-1)}{2}.
\end{equation}

\paragraph{Definition of the cross-moment terms}

For two covariates $a$ and $b$, the group-specific second cross-moment is defined as

\begin{equation}
\overline{x_a x_b}_{\,g}
=
\frac{1}{n_g}
\sum_{i \in g}
x_{i a}\, x_{i b},
\end{equation}

where $x_{i a}$ denotes the value of covariate $a$ for individual $i$,
and $n_g$ is the number of individuals in group $g$.

The corresponding overall (pooled) cross-moment is

\begin{equation}
\overline{x_a x_b}_{\,\cdot}
=
\frac{1}{N}
\sum_{i=1}^{N}
x_{i a}\, x_{i b},
\end{equation}

where $N$ is the total number of individuals across all groups.

The term
\begin{equation}
\left(
\overline{x_a x_b}_{\,g}
-
\overline{x_a x_b}_{\,\cdot}
\right)^2
\end{equation}
